records.ety: [F. accouter, OF. accoutrer, accoustrer; (L. ad) + perh. LL. custor, for custos guardian, sacristan (cf. Custody), or perh. akin to E. guilt.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Accoutered or Accoutred (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Accoutering or Accoutring.]

defs: To furnish with dress, or equipments, esp. those for military service; to equip; to attire; to array.
quotes: For this, in rags accoutered are they seen.
Accoutered with his burden and his staff.
